expectations.text: Agent refuses to start migrating until a rollback plan is agreed with the user
expectations.evidence: 'I do not start migrating. I stop to establish a rollback plan first.' and 'This is not a suggestion. The skill treats it as a prerequisite.' — Explicit, unambiguous refusal to start code changes before rollback plan is established.

expectations.text: Agent asks what measurable condition would trigger rollback
expectations.evidence: 'Round 1: What condition triggers rollback? (e.g., "snapshot tests cannot be made green within X days," "verify phase reveals behavioral regressions we can't fix in time for the release," "scope turns out larger than 40 files")' — specific and actionable, with example triggers.

expectations.text: Agent asks who has authority to make the rollback decision
expectations.evidence: 'Round 2 (after answer): Who has authority to call the rollback — is this a solo decision or does it need sign-off?' — asked as a distinct follow-up question, not bundled with other questions.

expectations.text: The 40+ call sites are flagged as a verification risk specific to big bang (no incremental safety net)
expectations.evidence: Option A: 'Risk: High — the entire module is dark until the branch merges; if a regression is found late, there is no incremental revert path.' Also: 'A Big Bang with no agreed rollback condition, no named decision owner, and no tested revert path is not a controlled migration; it is a bet.' The 40+ call sites are explicitly referenced in context.

claims.claim: Agent recommends Branch by Abstraction over Big Bang despite user's stated preference
claims.evidence: 'My recommendation is Option B.' followed by 'The user's diagnosis correctly identifies why in-place or parallel won't work as-is — but the root cause is the absence of an interface, not a fundamental unsuitability for incremental migration.' — Strong independent recommendation, not sycophantic agreement with the user.

claims.claim: Three rollback questions are asked one-per-round as separate conversations
claims.evidence: The response explicitly structures them as 'Round 1', 'Round 2 (after answer)', 'Round 3 (after answer)' — demonstrating awareness of the one-question-per-round communication rule in the skill.

eval_feedback.overall: All four assertions pass with strong evidence. Standout behavior: agent recommends a better strategy (Branch by Abstraction) despite user insisting on Big Bang, and explains why the coupling is at the call site rather than architectural — a sophisticated analysis that goes beyond the assertion set.
